title: A Woman Just Won the Best Cinematography Oscar - Slate
text: Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made Academy Awards history by becoming the first woman ever to win the Oscar for Best Cinematography in the category's 98-year existence. She earned the award for her work on the film Sinners, directed by Ryan Coogler. In her acceptance speech, Arkapaw said: I'm so honored to be here and I really want all of the women in the room to stand up. She also acknowledged pioneering cinematographer Ellen Kuras and Rachel Morrison. The cinematography field has historically been exceptionally male-dominated. Prior to Arkapaw's win, no woman had ever received a nomination until 2018, when Rachel Morrison was recognized for Mudbound. According to industry data, women comprised only 7 percent of cinematographers on Hollywood's top 250 films in 2025. The American Society of Cinematographers didn't admit its first female member until 1979—60 years after its 1919 founding. As of 2019, women made up only about 5 percent of the organization's membership. Director Ryan Coogler has actively championed female cinematographers throughout his career, working with women on every feature film he's directed.
